Skip to contents
razer 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference
  • Articles
  • Changelog

Package index

Model runner

One call to build, seed, and run a closed-population agent-based model (SI / SEI / SIS / SEIS / SIR / SEIR / SIRS / SEIRS) on the Column kernels.

run_model()
Run a closed-population agent-based model.

Agent & node storage

Rust-owned, dtype-tagged property arrays (per-agent and per-node/per-tick), the incremental-census carry-forward helpers, and the epidemic state codes.

Column
A Rust-owned, dtype-tagged property array (1-D scalar, or a 2-D vector report).
allocate_scalar()
Allocate a fresh, zero-filled property array of a given type and length.
allocate_vector()
Allocate a fresh, zero-filled 2-D property array (a per-slot report buffer).
carry_forward()
Carry a per-node counter forward one tick: copy column tick onto tick + 1.
carry_forward_states()
Carry census counters forward, and optionally total some of them.
laser_states()
Named integer vector of epidemic state codes.

Distributions

Parameterized probability distributions used to draw state-timer durations (e.g. incubation and infectious periods).

Distribution
A parameterized probability distribution that can be sampled repeatedly.
dist_beta()
Create a beta distribution on the open interval (0, 1).
dist_constant()
Create a degenerate (constant) distribution that always returns value.
dist_exp()
Create an exponential distribution with rate rate.
dist_gamma()
Create a gamma distribution parameterized by shape and scale.
dist_logistic()
Create a logistic distribution with the given location and scale.
dist_lognormal()
Create a log-normal distribution.
dist_normal()
Create a normal (Gaussian) distribution.
dist_poisson()
Create a Poisson distribution with rate (mean) lambda.
dist_uniform()
Create a continuous uniform distribution on the half-open interval [low, high).

Transmission & disease dynamics

Per-tick Column kernels for force of infection and the timed state transitions of the SI/SEI/SIS/SEIS/SIR/SEIR/SIRS/SEIRS menagerie. The kernels return per-node counts the model applies to its census via move_count; place calc_foi so the effective reproduction number is the full beta * D (see the modeling note in the package README / CLAUDE.md).

calc_foi()
Compute the per-node force of infection (FOI) for one tick.
transmission()
Stochastic transmission S→to_state, returning new infections per node.
transmission_si()
Stochastic transmission S→I into an ABSORBING I (the SI model), returning new infections per node.
step_si()
Advance M→S (maternal waning) and E→I (incubation) for one tick — SI / SEI.
step_sir()
Advance M→S, E→I, and I→absorbing_state for one tick — SIS / SIR / SEIS / SEIR.
step_sirs()
Advance M→S, E→I, I→R (with waning immunity), and R→S for one tick — SIRS / SEIRS.
step_timer_expire()
Generic timed transition from_state -> to_state into an UNTIMED destination.
step_timer_expire_set()
Generic timed transition from_state -> to_state into a TIMED destination.
move_count()
Apply a per-node transition count to the census at tick + 1.

Vital dynamics & importation

Births, deaths, and externally-seeded cases.

births()
Apply crude-birth-rate births for one tick; newborns enter maternal immunity (M).
mortality()
Apply natural mortality for one tick, returning deaths per node by state.
constant_pop_vitals_sir()
Apply constant-population SIR vital dynamics for one tick.
import_infections()
Import new infectious cases from a schedule, activating reserved agent slots.

Demographics & initialization

Realistic age structure and dates of death, and capacity sizing.

AliasedDistribution
A discrete distribution over bin indices 0..n, sampled by the Vose alias method.
aliased_distribution()
Build an AliasedDistribution from a vector of non-negative bin counts.
KaplanMeierEstimator
A Kaplan–Meier sampler over a cumulative-deaths-by-year life table.
kaplan_meier_estimator()
Build a KaplanMeierEstimator from cumulative deaths by year.
load_pyramid_csv()
Load a population-pyramid CSV into a numeric matrix.
sample_pyramid_ages()
Sample realistic per-agent ages (in days) from a population pyramid.
calc_capacity()
Estimate the agent capacity needed for a growing population.
calc_capacity_cdr()
Estimate the agent capacity for a growing population reclaimed with squash().

Spatial coupling & migration

Pairwise distances and migration-network models for spatial coupling of the force of infection.

distances()
Great-circle distance matrix between geographic points.
gravity()
Gravity migration-network model.
radiation()
Radiation migration-network model (Simini et al., Nature 2012).
stouffer()
Stouffer's intervening-opportunities migration model (Stouffer, 1940).
competing_destinations()
Competing-destinations migration model (Fotheringham, 1984).
row_normalizer()
Cap each row sum of a network matrix at max_rowsum.

Reproducibility

Seed the simulation RNG. After set_seed(), a run is reproducible regardless of CPU/thread count; unset_seed() reverts to entropy-seeded (random) runs.

set_seed()
Set the global random seed, making subsequent razer runs reproducible.
unset_seed()
Revert to a non-reproducible, entropy-seeded RNG (undo set_seed()).

Utilities

Grid broadcasting and binning helpers.

values_map()
Build a values map: broadcast a value into a n_ticks x n_nodes grid Column.
bincount()
Count occurrences of each value, NumPy bincount-style, into a buffer.
bincount_wt()
Weighted bincount: sum each element's weight into its bin.
bincount_where()
Count, per group, the agents whose property satisfies a comparison.
bincount_where_wt()
Sum a weight, per group, over the agents whose property satisfies a comparison.
squash()
Compact a people environment, dropping excluded agents and reclaiming their slots.

On this page

Developed by Christopher Lorton.
